note: Mathlib/Topology/GDelta/Basic.lean:63 -- def IsGδ (s : Set X) : Prop := ∃ T : Set (Set X), (∀ t ∈ T, IsOpen t) ∧ T.Countable ∧ s = ⋂₀ T; docstring 'a set s is a Gδ set if it can be represented as an intersection of countably many open sets'. This IS the definition of the Gδ-set concept.

Field-level match: 'Nevanlinna theory' = 'Value Distribution Theory'. The directory (Cartan.lean, CharacteristicFunction.lean, FirstMainTheorem.lean, Proximity/, LogCounting/) formalizes the characteristic (Nevanlinna height), proximity, and counting functions and the First Main Theorem; FirstMainTheorem.lean docstring explicitly titles it 'The First Main Theorem of Value Distribution Theory' citing Noguchi-Winkelmann's 'Nevanlinna Theory in Several Complex Variables...'. Path verified as a key under libraries.Mathlib.modules.Analysis.sub.Complex.sub.ValueDistribution in catalog/data/hierarchy.json.
